glossary.def: A generative model that learns to reverse a fixed noising process: training adds Gaussian noise to data and a network learns to predict and remove it, so sampling starts from pure static and iteratively denoises a structured image out of it over a few dozen steps. Stable to train and mode-covering, unlike GANs.

The diffusion training loss in which the network ε_θ(x_t, t) predicts the exact noise that was added at a randomly chosen timestep, rather than the clean image. Because the target noise is unit-variance at every step, this regression is far easier to optimize than predicting pixels directly.

Running the diffusion process inside a compressed latent space produced by a VAE encoder, instead of on raw pixels. With Stable Diffusion's 8× downsampling and 4 latent channels the tensor shrinks ~48×, cutting per-step cost enough to run text-to-image on a single consumer GPU. The decoder restores pixels only once, at the end.

A sampling technique that runs the diffusion model both with and without the caption and extrapolates along their difference by a guidance scale s. Higher s increases prompt adherence at the cost of diversity (and, too high, of realism); s=1 is plain conditional sampling, s=0 ignores the prompt. No separate classifier is required.

A diffusion denoiser that replaces the convolutional U-Net with a transformer operating on patches of the (latent) image or video. It scales more cleanly with compute and is the backbone of Stable Diffusion 3 and Sora-class video models.

An autoencoder whose bottleneck snaps each latent vector to the nearest entry in a learned discrete codebook, turning an image into a sequence of integer tokens. This makes images consumable by autoregressive or masked transformers; reconstruction quality of the codebook caps the achievable fidelity.

A non-autoregressive image generator that predicts many image tokens in parallel and reveals only the most confident ones each round, re-masking and repeating. It reaches a quality comparable to fast diffusion in roughly 8–12 rounds instead of hundreds of sequential autoregressive steps.

The unit of Sora-class video generation: a video is compressed into small cubes spanning a few pixels and a few frames, and a diffusion transformer denoises the whole grid jointly. Attending across space and time together enforces temporal coherence, but its O(N²) cost is why long, high-resolution video is the hard part.

refs.cite: Ho, J., Jain, A. & Abbeel, P. (2020). Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models. NeurIPS 2020.
Rombach, R., Blattmann, A., Lorenz, D., Esser, P. & Ommer, B. (2022). High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models (Stable Diffusion). CVPR 2022.
Ramesh, A., Dhariwal, P., Nichol, A., Chu, C. & Chen, M. (2022). Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents (DALL·E 2).
Ho, J. & Salimans, T. (2022). Classifier-Free Diffusion Guidance.
Saharia, C. et al. (2022). Photorealistic Text-to-Image Diffusion Models with Deep Language Understanding (Imagen).
Peebles, W. & Xie, S. (2023). Scalable Diffusion Models with Transformers (DiT). ICCV 2023.
Chang, H., Zhang, H., Jiang, L., Liu, C. & Freeman, W. T. (2022). MaskGIT: Masked Generative Image Transformer. CVPR 2022.
Brooks, T. et al. (2024). Video Generation Models as World Simulators (Sora). OpenAI.
